tools.note: The handler accepts only a canonical set ID and an absolute readable regular-file path, creates one immutable snapshot before network I/O, and binds the POST reservation to that snapshot's exact fileName, fileSize, and optional returned checksum. Transfer requires exact POST 201, required document links.self, canonical appScreenshots identity, AWAITING_UPLOAD, and requested-set proof: contradictory returned linkage is rejected, while omitted optional linkage is confirmed through the fully paginated parent-set inventory before transfer. Resource links.self is optional but must match the configured origin and exact resource path when present; every continuation is scoped likewise, and root-relative links are accepted. The same snapshot bytes produce the checksum receipt and commit body, and only PATCH 200 is a confirmed commit response. Any returned post-commit fileName, fileSize, or checksum must still match the snapshot. A semantic conflict in a confirmed PATCH 200 response is latched and cannot be converted into success by a later valid GET. COMPLETE is successful, FAILED is terminal failure, and other states remain processing-pending. Ambiguous reservation outcomes expose a 200-item parent inspection with next_url guidance and a fileName/fileSize candidate fingerprint; the local checksum receipt is separate and is not a server-side candidate field.

operations.condition: Repeated once per local file to reserve each asset.

tools.note: The batch is preflight-atomic only: all 1...50 unique paths must be absolute readable regular files and every immutable snapshot must be ready before the first Apple reservation. Remote operations are deliberately sequential and per-item, not remotely atomic; earlier committed items are not rolled back when a later item fails. Each item requires exact POST 201 and PATCH 200 responses, required document links.self, canonical identity, snapshot-matching fileName/fileSize/optional checksum, and AWAITING_UPLOAD. Contradictory set linkage is rejected; omitted optional linkage is confirmed through the fully paginated parent-set inventory before transfer. Resource links.self is optional but exact when present, and continuation links stay on the configured origin and endpoint scope. Returned post-commit snapshot evidence is validated when present. A semantic conflict in a confirmed PATCH 200 response is latched and cannot be laundered into success by later reconciliation. COMPLETE, FAILED, pending, rollback, and ambiguous-recovery outcomes are reported independently per item.

Redacted diagnostic for rejected, terminal, or unresolved upload outcomes.
Canonical known reservation ID on retained or failed outcomes.
Canonical known preview reservation ID for executable follow-up.
False only when failure is proven to precede reservation creation.
Whether a known pre-commit reservation was confirmed deleted.
Rejected, unknown, or committed-unverified reservation classification.
Whether an ambiguous reservation outcome produced a canonical resource ID.
True only for a confirmed accepted-but-unverified reservation, commit, or cleanup response.
True when reservation or cleanup commitment cannot be determined.
True when exact-resource or full parent-set inspection is required before retry.
Unexpected successful Apple status retained for recovery classification.

tools.note: The handler accepts only a canonical set ID and an absolute readable regular-file path, snapshots the video before network I/O, and binds the reservation to that snapshot's fileName, fileSize, and optional returned checksum. Transfer requires exact POST 201, required document links.self, canonical appPreviews identity, AWAITING_UPLOAD videoDeliveryState (deprecated assetDeliveryState fallback), and requested-set proof: contradictory linkage is rejected, while omitted optional linkage is confirmed through the fully paginated parent-set inventory before transfer. Resource links.self is optional but exact when present; every continuation remains on the configured origin and endpoint scope. The same bytes produce the separate checksum receipt and commit body, and only PATCH 200 confirms the commit response; returned post-commit snapshot evidence is validated when present. A semantic conflict in a confirmed PATCH 200 response is latched and cannot be converted into success by a later valid GET. Nullable previewFrameTimeCode preserves omission, explicit null, and value; mimeType preserves its compatibility default and explicit null. Ambiguous reservations use a 200-item parent inspection with next_url and fileName/fileSize candidate matching, never checksum as a server-side candidate field.
